_comment: Copy to ~/Library/Application Support/weft/app.json. The canonical shape declares clusters[] (one entry per weft cluster, i.e. one etcd quorum) with nested dcs[] (one per machine room inside the cluster). Failover lives within a cluster ; switching between clusters is operator-driven (federation). The legacy flat endpoints[] shape is still accepted and auto-wrapped into a single 'default' cluster.
_auth_comment: OPTIONAL : omit the whole `auth` block to skip the sign-in window (preserves the dev / SSH-tunnel-only flow). When present, the app shows a 2-button picker (OpenPubkey / OIDC) before bringing up the tray, caches the resulting session token in macOS Keychain, and stamps Bearer on every WebView API call.
_auth_keypair_comment: OPTIONAL dev-only third auth method. Set `keypair_fallback: true` AND `gateway` to add a 3rd button (Sign in with local key (dev)) to the picker. On click, the app loads (or generates + persists) an ed25519 private key in macOS Keychain (service=weft-app-keypair), signs a 60-second assertion bound to gateway/api/auth/keypair, and POSTs it ; the server returns a session id_token if the matching public key is in its --keypair-allowlist file. Register your pubkey by running `weft-app-osx --print-pubkey` and pasting the output into the server's allowlist (entries[].pubkey). NEVER enable this against an Internet-facing production cluster — the server-side flag should also be unset there.

dcs.name: salle-jaures
dcs._loom_note: OPTIONAL : when this DC also runs a weft-loom microVM (deployment.type=ha, role=loom), expose its listener via the same bastion's SSH-forward and set loom_addr. The shell builds a second supervisor + gateway pair pointing here ; the tray's 'Open Loom' menu item shows a stable loopback origin that fails over silently between DCs.

dcs.name: salle-tolbiac
dcs._note: Use 'direct' when the device is already on the WireGuard mesh ; the webui listener binds the mesh interface only. When loom is also reachable on the mesh, set loom_addr to its mesh-IP:port.

clusters.name: tokyo
clusters._note: Second cluster, geographically distant — different etcd quorum, federated with paris through the cluster catalogue. Manual switching only ; today's flat failover pool still includes its DCs but tomorrow's per-cluster supervisor will isolate the choice to the active cluster.

_legacy_endpoints_note: Older configs that declared a flat endpoints[] without clusters[] still load — they're wrapped into a single 'default' cluster at load time. Migrate to clusters[].dcs[] for cluster-aware menubar labels.
